2604.05830.title: "OK Aura, Be Fair With Me": Demographics-Agnostic Training for Bias Mitigation in Wake-up Word Detection
2604.05830.paper_summary: Voice-based interfaces are widely used; however, achieving fair Wake-up Word detection across diverse speaker populations remains a critical challenge due to persistent demographic biases. This study evaluates the effectiveness of demographics-agnostic training techniques in mitigating performance disparities among speakers of varying sex, age, and accent. We utilize the OK Aura database for our experiments, employing a training methodology that excludes demographic labels, which are reserved for evaluation purposes. We explore (i) data augmentation techniques to enhance model generalization and (ii) knowledge distillation of pre-trained foundational speech models. The experimental results indicate that these demographics-agnostic training techniques markedly reduce demographic bias, leading to a more equitable performance profile across different speaker groups. Specifically, one of the evaluated techniques achieves a Predictive Disparity reduction of 39.94\% for sex, 83.65\% for age, and 40.48\% for accent when compared to the baseline. This study highlights the effectiveness of label-agnostic methodologies in fostering fairness in Wake-up Word detection.
2604.05830.paper_summary_zh: 語音介面被廣泛使用；然而，由於持續存在的人口統計偏見，在不同說話者群體中實現公平的喚醒詞檢測仍然是一個關鍵挑戰。本研究評估了無關人口統計的訓練技術在減少不同性別、年齡和口音的說話者之間性能差異方面的有效性。我們利用 OK Aura 數據庫進行實驗，採用不包含人口統計標籤的訓練方法，這些標籤僅用於評估目的。我們探討了 (i) 數據增強技術以提高模型的泛化能力，以及 (ii) 對預訓練基礎語音模型的知識蒸餾。實驗結果表明，這些無關人口統計的訓練技術顯著減少了人口統計偏見，導致不同說話者群體之間的性能更為公平。具體來說，評估的技術之一在性別方面實現了 39.94\% 的預測差異減少，在年齡方面為 83.65\%，在口音方面為 40.48\%，與基準相比。本研究凸顯了無標籤方法在促進喚醒詞檢測公平性方面的有效性。
2604.05830.author: Fernando López et.al.
2604.05830.authors: Fernando López, Paula Delgado-Santos, Pablo Gómez, David Solans, Jordi Luque
